title: Nonlinear Schrodinger Equations for Dense Bose Fluid and He4 Film at Low Temperatures
abstract: We have derived the nonlinear Schrodinger equation generalizing the Gross-Pitaevskii (GP) equa tion for dilute Bose gas. The derivation is based on the Hartree-Fock time-dependent mean-field theory with an arbitrary intermolecular interaction potential. It is shown that obtained nonlinear Schrodinger equation with appropriate redefinition of coefficients can be used for description of dense Bose fluid at low temperatures. We also present the Schrodinger type equation describing the superfluid component of helium in two fluid hydrodynamics. This approach leads to quantum correction for superfluid component of velocity in two fluid hydrodynamics. We also have derived the nonlinear Schrodinger equation for superfluid He4 film at low temperatures. It is shown that this Schrodinger type equation for superfluid He4 film leads to phonon-roton dispersion relation for elementary excitation at low temperatures.
summary: This paper develops generalized nonlinear Schrödinger equations to describe the quantum dynamics of Bose fluids, including dense Bose gases and superfluid Helium-4. By linearizing these equations, the authors successfully derive the characteristic phonon-roton dispersion relation for superfluid films. The work provides a comprehensive theoretical framework connecting mean-field theory to observable excitation spectra in quantum fluids.
main_problem: The paper derives and applies generalized nonlinear Schrödinger equations (NLSE) to model the dynamics of Bose fluids, specifically dense Bose gas and superfluid Helium-4 films at low temperatures.
main_result: The analysis yields the phonon-roton dispersion relation for superfluid He4 films and provides generalized NLSE forms applicable to both dilute and dense Bose fluids.
method: The methodology involves using the Hartree-Fock time-dependent mean-field theory, linearizing the resulting NLSE to study elementary excitations, and deriving coupled hydrodynamic equations for the two-fluid model.
model_or_system: The system under study includes dilute Bose gases, dense Bose fluids (like liquid He4), and superfluid He4 films. The theoretical framework relies on deriving and analyzing nonlinear Schrödinger equations derived from many-body Hamiltonians.
key_observables: Phonon-roton dispersion relation, superfluid velocity corrections, and the energy spectrum of elementary excitations $\epsilon(p)$.
important_parameters: Phonon velocity ($c$), roton minimum energy ($\Delta$), and the coupling parameters ($F_n$ or $\lambda_n$) derived from these physical constants.
main_assumptions: Approximations include neglecting higher-order gradient terms in the dilute gas limit and assuming the validity of the mean-field approximation for the Bose fluid.
figures_summary: The notes detail the derivation of dispersion relations $\epsilon(p)$ and the final form of the energy spectrum $E(k)$ as a polynomial in $k^2$, relating it to the coefficients $G,  eta, \sigma$.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from deriving the generalized NLSE for dilute Bose gas, extending it to dense Bose fluids, then analyzing the two-fluid hydrodynamics for superfluid He4, and finally focusing on the specific NLSE and dispersion relation for superfluid He4 films.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant as it applies advanced mean-field quantum field theory techniques (NLSE) to model superfluid dynamics, which is a cornerstone topic in AMO physics and quantum gases research.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
